name: Noise Filtering (Surprise Gate)
description: Tests that NeuroMem's surprise-gated memory filters out small talk and noise, only storing the one high-signal fact buried in 10 turns of chit-chat.

conversation.content: Pretty good, the weather is nice today.
That's great to hear! Nice weather always makes the day better.
Yeah, totally. So how are you doing?
I'm doing well, thanks for asking! What's on your mind today?
Not much, just chilling. Had some coffee earlier.
A good cup of coffee always hits the spot! Anything else going on?
Oh wait, I forgot to mention — I just got accepted into MIT's PhD program in machine learning! Starting next fall.
That's absolutely incredible — congratulations! MIT's ML program is world-class. What research area are you most excited about?
Thanks! Anyway, yeah the weather really is something today.
It sure is! Enjoy the beautiful day — and congratulations again on MIT!
Haha thanks. Ok bye for now!
Take care! Talk to you soon.

test_queries.query: What important news does this user have?
test_queries.description: Should recall the MIT PhD acceptance — the ONLY high-signal fact in 14 turns of mostly noise.

rag_expected_failure: Standard RAG indexes all 7 user turns equally, flooding the vector DB with 'weather is nice' and 'had some coffee' with similar priority to the MIT acceptance.
neuromem_advantage: Surprise scorer assigns low novelty (< 0.3) to small talk and high novelty (> 0.8) to the MIT acceptance, gating 85%+ of noise writes
